title: branch — subprocessors
description: Geocoding providers process the tenant's structured address + return coordinates. Only engaged when tenant has `branch_geocoding` entitlement (Medium+). Map tile rendering (for the map-view SDUI screen) engages one additional subprocessor per tenant selection — those tiles are billed to the platform.

subprocessors.name: Google Maps Geocoding API
subprocessors.purpose: Resolve tenant-supplied structured address → coordinates (lat/lng/accuracy_m) + canonical formatted address. Highest-quality provider for most global addresses.
data_processed: The tenant's structured address (line1, line2, city, subdivision, postal_code, country_code)
The Branch ULID (as an opaque token in the request metadata; used for the API cost-attribution log)
subprocessors.location: US default; EU processing via `region=EU` parameter when the tenant's Region.data_residency_zone = EU
subprocessors.when_engaged: config('branch.geocoding.provider') === 'google_maps' AND tenant has branch_geocoding entitlement
subprocessors.retention_note: Google may cache request payloads for up to 30 days per their DPA. The Branch ULID we ship as metadata is opaque — reverse-linkage to tenant identity is impossible from the payload alone.

subprocessors.name: Mapbox Geocoding
subprocessors.purpose: Alternative geocoding provider. Moderate quality, developer-friendly rate limits, competitive pricing.
subprocessors.location: US default (Mapbox); EU-region processing available via a separate endpoint config

subprocessors.name: Nominatim (OpenStreetMap)
subprocessors.purpose: Free community geocoding service. Aggressive rate limits (1 req/s per Nominatim usage policy). Suitable for small-tier tenants without heavy geocoding needs OR for tenants running a private Nominatim instance in their own infra.
data_processed: A contact email in the User-Agent header (required by Nominatim usage policy)
subprocessors.rate_limit_note: The public Nominatim tile is rate-limited to 1 req/s. Tenants with heavy geocoding needs should self-host Nominatim + configure the endpoint override — the branch module falls back to per-tenant throttling to keep within the policy.

subprocessors.name: Mapbox Static Maps
subprocessors.purpose: Render the map-view SDUI screen's tile layer. Tiles are fetched by the client browser directly from Mapbox — the branch module never proxies tiles.
data_processed: The branch's coordinates (in the tile-request URL)
The end-user's IP address (browser-to-Mapbox direct connection)
subprocessors.note: The Mapbox access token is a platform-scoped token, NOT per-tenant. Tenants that require self-hosted tiles for regulatory reasons can configure `branch.map_view.tile_provider` to a custom URL (deferred to Wave 3).

notes: Nominatim + OpenStreetMap tiles are 'best effort' — Nominatim explicitly disclaims any SLA on the public tile. The branch module's `branch.geocoding_enabled` kill switch is the mitigation.
The map-view tile provider is DISTINCT from the geocoding provider — a tenant can pick Google Maps for geocoding + accept the platform-default Mapbox tiles for the map view (or vice versa).

GDPR Art. 44 (cross-border transfers): each provider's DPA covers the transfer legal basis. EU-region tenants who require EU-only processing configure Google Maps with `region=EU` OR pick Nominatim (OSM Foundation is EU-based).
